ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Continued To Mar-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For 1) Order Compelling Trust Accounting; 2) Order Compelling Accounting By Attorney In Fact; 3) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; 4) Professional
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Continued To Mar-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Sipos, via Zoom, for George R. Woodhouse and Barry S. Woodhouse, Petitioners; Sheila Robello, via Zoom, for Gary Botto and Botto Law Group, Respondents; Patricia Bonheyo, via Zoom, for Michael Helms and Helms & Associates CPA's, LLP, Respondents.

The court advises that it appears as though additional settlement discussion seems appropriate for this matter and that the court is inclined to order the parties to participate in mediation again. Mr. Sipos advises of being amenable to additional mediation but is only willing to do so if some conditions are met beforehand and advises that setting a trial date might be a good idea to move this matter forward. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the court's tentative ruling to order the parties to participate in additional mediation with Ret. Judge Honorable James Lambden. Upon consideration by the court, the parties are ordered to additional mediation as stated above, without any conditions.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Mar-08-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Counsel is to complete mediation by Feb-22-2023 and to file a Joint Case Management Statement by Mar-03-2023. The matter is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on Sep-11-2023 at 9:30 a.m. Order for the same signed in open court. A copy of the order and notice of trial will be provided to Counsel electronically.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Aug-03-2022 Continued To Dec-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Sipos, via Zoom, for George R. Woodhouse and Barry S. Woodhouse, Petitioners; Vincent Yu, via Zoom, for Gary Botto and Botto Law Group, Respondents; Pat Bonheyo, via Zoom, for Michael Helms and Helms & Associates CPA's, LLP, Respondents.

Mr. Sipos reports of the parties mediating some time ago and of his clients attempts to marshal assets. Mr. Sipos advises that Mr. Yu has provided him with records and noticed that there appears to be one million dollars that is not accounted for. Mr. Yu advises that he intends to discuss the preceding with Mr. Sipos. The court advises that it will be issuing a Case Management Order and offers a brief description of what the parties can expect within the order.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Dec-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Case Management Hearing is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Off Calendar May-26-2021 Continued To Aug-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For 1) Order Compelling Trust Accounting; 2) Order Compelling Accounting By Attorney In Fact; 3) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; 4) Professional
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Continued To May-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello, Esq., counsel for Gary Botto, Trustee (appearing by video conference); Kevin Rodriguez, Esq. for Barry Woodhouse and Ronald Woodhouse, beneficiaries (appearing by video conference); Patricia Bonheyo, Esq. counsel for Michael Helms and Helms & Associates, Respondents (appearing by video conference).

The Court recites the purpose of today's hearing, including the request for an accounting and a request to suspend the current trustee, Mr. Botto. At the Court's invitation, Ms. Robello responds to the request, noting the procedural irregularities that occasioned the withdrawal of the accounting petition and the assertion that no accounting was required by the trust document.

Mr. Rodriguez responds, advising the Court that the petitioners have commenced discovery, including the propagation of third-party subpoenas and requests for document production. He further contends that a neutral trustee is necessary due to the excessive fees that the current trustee has billed. Ms. Robello and Mr. Rodriguez offer further argument on the matter.

The Court questions whether replacement of the trustee is efficacious at this time and proposes a modification wherein no accounting fees or legal fees could be paid without Court approval. Mr. Rodriguez would accept the modification without waiver of any other remedies. Ms. Robello notes that there may arise occasions wherein expenses would need to be paid on a more urgent basis and proposes that such expenses be paid upon approval by Mr. Rodriguez's clients. After discussion, Mr. Rodriguez accepts the additional modification. The Court is to prepare the Order. It is noted that First Republic Bank, as a possible successor Trustee, should be notified of the proceedings. Mr. Rodriguez will attend to that notice.

The Court continues the current matter to May 26, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
